label: Multi-objective, constrained & Pareto optimization

node.label: Multi-objective, constrained & Pareto optimization
node.definition: Multiple competing goals and hard constraints are optimized jointly rather than collapsed into one unconstrained score.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Multi-objective, constrained & Pareto optimization is to connect Optimization theory, Game theory & decision theory to downstream capabilities including Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment.
examples: substrate for Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment
limitations: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Optimization theory, Game theory & decision theory.

dependencies.rationale: Multi-objective, constrained & Pareto optimization uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Multi-objective, constrained & Pareto optimization uses Game theory & decision theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Multi-objective, constrained & Pareto optimization uses Labels, targets, rewards & preferences as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
